PLAINTIFF HEATHER THOMPSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES THE UNOPPOSED MOTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT IS TO PROVIDE CODE COMPLIANT FURTHER RESPONSES WITHIN 30 DAYS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES 12.4 (SPECIFYING THE NUMBER OF PHOTOGRAPHS IN DEFENDANT'S POSSESSION, CUSTODY OR CONTROL, IDENTIFYING WHO TOOK THEM AND WHEN, CONFIRMING THAT ALL OF THEM HAVE BEEN PRODUCED TO PLAINTIFF, AND PROVIDING ANY INFORMATION AVAILABLE TO DEFENDANT REGARDING THE IDENTITY OF THE NEIGHBOR WHO MAY HAVE TAKEN PHOTOGRAPHS), 15.1 (STATING WHETHER DEFENDANT DENIES THAT (A) DEFENDANT'S ACTS (I) WERE NEGLIGENT, (II) WERE THE LEGAL (PROXIMATE) CAUSE OF INJURIES AND DAMAGES TO PLAINTIFF, (III) OCCURRED ON PALM AVENUE NEAR ITS INTERSECTION WITH CALIFORNIA STREET IN SAN FRANCISCO, (B) PLAINTIFF SUFFERED THE ITEMS IDENTIFIED IN PARAGRAPH 11 OF THE COMPLAINT, AND IF SO DESCRIBING THE FACTS UPON WHICH THE DENIAL IS BASED AND IDENTIFYING ANY PERSONS HAVING KNOWLEDGE OF THOSE FACTS AND ANY DOCUMENTS KNOWN TO DEFENDANT THAT SUPPORT THE DENIAL, AND ALSO DESCRIBING THE FACTS UPON WHICH DEFENDANT BASES HER ASSERTION THAT "THE FORCE OF THE IMPACT APPEARS INSUFFICIENT TO HAVE CAUSE PLAINTIFF INJURY AND IDENTIFYING ANY PERSONS HAVING KNOWLEDGE OF THOSE FACTS AND ANY DOCUMENTS KNOWN TO DEFENDANT THAT SUPPORT THE ASSERTION), 16.2 (DESCRIBING THE FACTS UPON WHICH DEFENDANT BASES HER ASSERTION THAT PLAINTIFF WAS NOT INJURED IN THE INCIDENT AND IDENTIFYING ANY PERSONS HAVING KNOWLEDGE OF THOSE FACTS AND ANY DOCUMENTS KNOWN TO DEFENDANT THAT SUPPORT THE ASSERTION). DETAILS SEE MINI MINUTES. JUDGE PRO TEM ROGER B MEAD, CLERK J SENGMANY, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Verified Responses To Interoggs
Off Calendar Jan-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: duplicate entry

Motion To Compel Further Verified Responses To Interoggs
Off Calendar Jan-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF HEATHER THOMPSON'S Motion To Compel Further Verified Responses To Interrogatories The unopposed motion is granted as follows: Defendant is to provide code compliant further responses within 30 days to Form Interrogatories 12.4 (specifying the number of photographs in defendant's possession, custody or control, identifying who took them and when, confirming that all of them have been produced to plaintiff, and providing any information available to defendant regarding the identity of the neighbor who may have taken photographs), 15.1 (stating whether defendant denies that (a) defendant's acts (i) were negligent, (ii) were the legal (proximate) cause of injuries and damages to plaintiff, (iii) occurred on Palm Avenue near its intersection with California Street in San Francisco, (b) plaintiff suffered the items identified in paragraph 11 of the complaint, and if so describing the facts upon which the denial is based and identifying any persons having knowledge of those facts and any documents known to defendant that support the denial, and also describing the facts upon which defendant bases her assertion that "the force of the impact appears insufficient to have cause plaintiff injury and identifying any persons having knowledge of those facts and any documents known to defendant that support the assertion), 16.2 (describing the facts upon which defendant bases her assertion that plaintiff was not injured in the incident and identifying any persons having knowledge of those facts and any documents known to defendant that support the assertion); Special Interrogatories 2-6 (describing any cell phone or other communication device that may have been in the vehicle with defendant at the time of the incident, providing the manufacturer(s), model number(s), cell phone service provider(s), person(s) whose names the cell phone service account(s) were in); RFPs 1-3, 7, 9, 10, 11, 16 -18 and 21 (modified to read "All WRITINGS reflecting use of a communication device that may have been in your vehicle on the day of the INCIDENT").The response to Special Interrogatory 9 is sufficient; the record on the motion is insufficient to establish whether further responses to RFPs 12-15 are warranted since the import of the qualifier "different than clamed" is not clear. Plaintiff shall provide verifications of her original and further discovery responses with her further responses. Sanctions of $1,288.50 are awarded against defendant's counsel for failing to provide proper discovery responses and failing to meet and confer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM ROGER B MEAD, CLERK J SENGMANY, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Motion To Compel Further Verified Responses To Interoggs
Off Calendar Jan-11-2017 Continued To Jan-31-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF HEATHER THOMPSON Motion To Compel Further Verified Responses To Interrogatories and demand to inspect is CONTINUED to January 31, 2017 at 9:00 AM in Department 302 in order to allow the moving party to provide moving papers in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. The moving party shall immediately provide the courtesy copies with department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter C. Catalanotti; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302/jpt)
